description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the ruby:${RUBY_VERSION} Docker image, defines a REDIS_URL variable, and runs a before_script that prints the Ruby version, shows the Ruby binary path, installs bundler via gem, and runs bundle install with parallel jobs. In the test stage add two integration jobs (integration_reliable and integration_semi) that extend a .integration template, execute in parallel for RUBY_VERSION 2.7 and 3.0, change to the tests/reliability directory, run bundle exec ruby reliability_test.rb, use the redis:alpine service, and set the JOB_FETCHER variable to reliable or semi respectively. Also in the test stage add a kill_interruption job that runs in parallel for the two Ruby versions, cd into tests/interruption, execute bundle exec ruby test_kill_signal.rb, and use the redis:alpine service. Add a term_interruption job that similarly runs in parallel for RUBY_VERSION 2.7 and 3.0, cd into tests/interruption, run bundle exec ruby test_term_signal.rb, and use the redis:alpine service. Finally add an rspec job that runs in parallel for the two Ruby versions, executes bundle exec rspec, uses the redis:alpine service, and always uploads the coverage/ directory as an artifact that expires in 31 days with a coverage regex pattern.
